decision.archive_summary: Current workstream is Codex context minor GC regression hardening. Earlier in the thread, the user asked to expand harder natural-Chinese multi-turn topic-shift cases into fixed regressions and avoid whitelist-style hardcoding. That was reported as implemented by adding a harder zh dense multitopic return live eval case and tightening the packaged guard so it cannot override a shadow-guard veto. Those older implementation turns can be compacted into pins while keeping the recent test/rerun turns raw.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn '继续' stays on the same active topic, so relation=continue. The immediate next turn most directly depends on the recent rerun/testing exchange (t8-t11), not the older already-completed edit confirmations. The earlier task intent and constraints still matter semantically, so preserve them as pins rather than raw turns. Off-topic or resolved status confirmations from t1-t7 are good eviction candidates.
